summary: A single-story rectangular commercial or institutional building with a flat roof featuring a central cupola. The structure uses brick and stone materials with prominent white stone pillars at corners and entrances. The building has a symmetrical facade with horizontal banding of materials and large window openings on the front. A dark red/nether brick roof with geometric pattern covers the top.
rebuild_hints: Start with a 14×10 rectangular footprint at ground level
Build walls 5 blocks high using brick as primary material
Add horizontal bands of stonebrick at regular intervals (approximately every 2 blocks vertically)
Place large white stone pillars at all four corners and flanking the main entrance
Install glass window panes in large openings on the front facade
Create a flat roof using stone blocks
Add a central cupola structure on top of the roof (approximately 2×2 blocks, 2 blocks tall)
Cover roof with nether brick in a geometric pattern (alternating block placement)
Add decorative fence railings around the roof perimeter and cupola
Place stone foundation blocks around the base perimeter
Ensure bilateral symmetry on the front-facing facade
uncertainties: Exact window dimensions and placement pattern not fully clear from all angles
Interior layout and room configuration cannot be determined from exterior views
Precise height of cupola relative to main roof unclear
Whether the nether brick roof is purely decorative or structural
Exact material composition of horizontal bands (could be varied stone types)
Depth dimension estimated from perspective; actual depth may vary by 1-2 blocks
Whether there are additional floors or basement levels not visible
